title: Hieracium juelii
intro: Hieracium juelii, also known as Juel's hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium juelii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium juelii is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
taxonomy: Hieracium juelii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a member of the subfamily Cichorioideae, which comprises various types of thistle and dandelion-like plants. The plant is also known as a hawkweed, which is a term used for various species within the genus Hieracium. The genus Hieracium is divided into several subgenera, with Hieracium juelii belonging to one of these subgroups. Further classification within the subgenus would depend on specific characteristics of the plant.
